corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: The encoder architecture g enc used in our experiments consists of a strided convolutional neural network that runs directly on the 16KHz PCM audio waveform.

top_candidates.sentence: In this work, we propose a universal unsupervised learning approach to extract useful representations from high-dimensional data, which we call Contrastive Predictive Coding.
These techniques made manually specified features largely redundant and have greatly improved state-of-the-art in several real-world applications .
In this paper we propose the following: first, we compress high-dimensional data into a much more compact latent embedding space in which conditional predictions are easier to model.

When predicting future information we instead encode the target x (future) and context c (present) into a compact distributed vector representations (via non-linear learned mappings) in a way that maximally preserves the mutual information of the original signals x and c defined as By maximizing the mutual information between the encoded representations (which is bounded by the MI between the input signals), we extract the underlying latent variables the inputs have in commmon.

In Computer Vision use a triplet loss on tracked video patches so that patches from the same object at different timesteps are more similar to each other than to random patches. propose to predict the relative postion of patches in an image and in color values are predicted from a greyscale images.
We present benchmarks on four different application domains: speech, images, natural language and reinforcement learning.
In this paper we presented Contrastive Predictive Coding (CPC), a framework for extracting compact latent representations to encode predictions over future observations.
